meanings.definition: people who are destined to die soon
examples: the agony of the doomed was in his voice
She was destined to become a great pianist

meanings.definition: pronounce a sentence on (somebody) in a court of law
examples: He was condemned to ten years in prison

meanings.definition: make certain of the failure or destruction of
examples: This decision will doom me to lose my position
the black spot told the old sailor he was doomed

meanings.definition: in danger of the eternal punishment of Hell
marked by or promising bad fortune; ; ; ; - W.H.Prescott
examples: their business venture was doomed from the start
the unlucky prisoner was again put in irons

meanings.definition: (usually followed by `to') determined by tragic fate
examples: fated to be the scene of Kennedy's assassination
